frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
generated_comment: 80k per month to someone who already has a job?? This is literally reverse dowry at this point. Bhai log wake up karo — for a lot of women marriage is just a financial upgrade option, and divorce is the exit with a salary attached. I'm a software engineer, I earn decent, so I'm automatically a target. No prenup = no marriage, simple. And before anyone calls me bitter, I have nothing against women, I just believe in reading the fine print before signing any contract — and marriage in India has the worst terms for men by far.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes covert misogyny by framing women as a class whose primary motivation for marriage is financial opportunism ('financial upgrade option'), reducing women's agency and intentions to mercenary calculation. It uses the 'reverse dowry' framing to naturalise grievance and presents itself as rational/neutral ('reading the fine print') while encoding a generalised distrust of women as a group.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
